key_points: Park Place apartments described as spacious and well-furnished
Roommate matching system is slow and unreliable for freshmen
Parking is expensive (~$120/year) with chronic shortage of spots
key_quotes: the rooms are pretty good and spacious!! the apartment itself is decently furnished
I didn't know I had a roommate til a week before move in
parking is a beast... you will never find parking

key_points: Queer students exist but acceptance varies heavily by major
Clubs, intramurals, and coffee shops are main socializing venues
Fraternities don't require rec letters; rush process is accessible
key_quotes: There's a good bit of queer students, though general acceptance can differ some by major
Go to a few interest meetings... you don't have to keep going, just go to one and find your people
Be cautious reaching out to TKE members. The ones now are asshats.

key_points: Cafeteria uses ID card swipes; unlimited plan allows visits hourly
Dining Bucks (DB) can be used at the caf for ~$9 per meal
Halal food options are very limited; one local Islamic center may help
key_quotes: If you have unlimited swipes you can go to the caf as often as every hour
I think tech farms meat is halal... I remember being surprised by the halal labeling
there isn't any market or halal restaurants [in town]

key_points: No direct mentions of counseling services from students
Architecture students report brutal workloads with multiple all-nighters
Academic probation and financial stress appear as recurring student concerns
key_quotes: He pulled multiple all nighters every quarter and he ended up dropping and switching to business
I'm on academic probation and probably going to lose my Sap
I was so tired after all the events [at orientation]

key_points: Bulldog Scholarship details not included in acceptance letter; check portal separately
Class purge anxiety is real — students fear dropped classes over payment confusion
FAFSA must be renewed every year; loans don't auto-renew
key_quotes: there should be a dedicated section in your admission portal showing a different letter notifying you that you've received it
Got an email saying my classes will be held and that they will be dropped if I don't start paying soon
Yes you have to reapply every year... you always have to fill out the FAFSA each year and then reapply with the university financial aide

key_points: Engineering programs are rigorous; MacBooks explicitly not recommended by COES
CS/Cyber double major is common due to overlapping coursework
Accounting 201 is harder than expected — skipping class leads to failure
key_quotes: MacBooks are not recommended, bold and underlined [by COES]
You'll fall if you don't go [to Accounting]. Accounting is not intuitive enough to BS your way through
Tech CS grad, class of 2018. Cyber/Electrical with CS double major was pretty common. But it's a heavy workload.

key_points: Housing office has limited hours and poor phone responsiveness
Cashier's office sends confusing or alarming emails about payment
International admissions department is slow to respond to scholarship questions
key_quotes: I tried calling housing, but they don't pick up
Asked for clarification due to this and was basically told the same thing twice
as of now, absolutely no response from the international admissions department

key_points: Ruston is a small, conservative town — limited dining, nightlife, and cultural options
Campus is walkable but roads have potholes; Walmart is within walking distance
Moving to Sun Belt conference seen as positive for regional rivalries and travel
key_quotes: Tech and Ruston in general is conservative so those face piercings are not going to work well for you
the roads are riddled with potholes
So glad we're leaving that discombobulated trash heap of a conference [CUSA]

key_points: Accounting grads have landed Big 4 firms internationally
CS and engineering grads report strong placement in cybersecurity and engineering roles
Architecture graduates warned they'll be lowest paid in the room long-term
key_quotes: My close friend got his bachelors and masters in accounting from Tech and now works at a Big 4 firm in Korea
2024 CS grad, working in cybersecurity the last 2 years
20 years later when you're in a room with engineers, project managers, and finance watchdogs you'll be the lowest paid and hardest working among that group

key_points: Free or near-free tuition via scholarships makes Tech a compelling engineering option
Long commutes to save money are common but unsustainable by mid-semester
Out-of-state students should compare carefully; in-state value is the real draw
key_quotes: If you aren't getting need based [aid] to make those expensive options affordable, I would go with free for almost every engineering field
I drove to a campus that was just 50 minutes from my house... it got real old by November
Keep in mind you won't actually be saving 10k with gas prices and the wear and tear on your car

key_points: Tour guides praised for being candid and honest, not scripted
Campus feels friendly and unpretentious compared to larger Louisiana schools
Strong alumni pride around notable names like Terry Bradshaw and Karl Malone
key_quotes: LA Tech felt like home. Everyone felt like they wanted to be there. The tour guides kept it 100 with me
Never once did I hear anyone on campus bash another college
An underrated G5 as far as facilities are concerned

key_points: **Campus Culture:** Ruston is openly conservative; LGBTQ+ and non-Christian students report discomfort
**Administration:** Class purge threats and confusing financial emails cause serious student anxiety
**Safety:** At least one student post flagged Ruston-adjacent Monroe as having notable crime concerns
key_quotes: Ever since I came to Ruston, feels like not a safe place for non Christians, non-MAGA, or non-white
Watch out for the police they are unforgiving
Monroe is a pretty dangerous area. You will need a car. I recommend purchasing a weapon for your home

key_points: Professional Aviation program is selective but accessible with PPL already earned
Athletics department hires students across many roles including video, photography, and marketing
Campus has explorable tunnels and rooftop access points — informal student tradition
key_quotes: Athletics is always hiring. There's game day production, marketing, sports information, video and camera crew, photography
your GPA shouldn't be an issue... you already have your PPL. There's a good chance you get accepted
There are tunnels [under campus]
